en.value:
Objective 1: Enhance knowledge, capacity and awareness of Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) education for children, parents and teachers

	
		Student and teacher Knowledge, Attitude, and Practice (KAP) survey
	
		
			Orientation and Capacity building of teachers and community resource persons 
	


	 

	 

	
		Orientation and formation of Junior Red Cross Circles (JRCs)
	
		
			First Aid Training of Teachers and Executive Members of JRCs
	


	 

	
		Training of JRCs (DRR, Leadership, Child to Chil
	
		Establishment of Emergency Funds
		 
	
		
			Formation of Parents Council to advocate DRR and safe schools and community environment
		
			 
	
	
		Enhancement of School Libraries


	Objective 2: Design and promote “safe school” standards by training and mobilizing parents, students, and teachers to champion school-based DRR

	
		
			Promoting schools as ‘Centers of Community DRR’
		
			Develop school ‘action plan’ on Disaster Preparedness and intervention
		
			Improve school facilities based on “safe school” standards 
		
			Equip schools with First Aid, Search &Rescue and Early Warning System tools and material
		
			Art/ Essay/ Speaking/ Drama Competitions
		
			School-Based Disaster Response Simulations
		
			JRC district jamboree
	
	
		Objective 3: Advocate for the integration of “safe school” standards and DRR curriculum into district-level education policies
	
		 
	
		
			Sign Project MoU with Key Government Stakeholders
		
			Lobby the District Education Office (DEO) and Ministry of Education to include DRR in the formal curriculum at both primary and secondary levels
		
			Work with the DEO to Develop “Safe School” Standards for Application across the District

en.full: Supporting the Role of Schools in Disaster Risk Reduction (SRSD)

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
